title: Exclusive | Justin Bieber's got a lot riding on Coachella comeback
description: Justin Bieber is gearing up for a comeback at Coachella — but there's a helluva lot on the line for the former teen heartthrob.
text: See more of our coverage in your search results. Add Page Six on Google Justin Bieber is gearing up for a comeback at Coachella — and there’s a helluva lot on the line for the former teen heartthrob. He’ll headline both Saturdays, April 11 and 18, at the Indio, California, festival, and recently gave 500 fans a glimpse of what to expect with an intimate warm-up show at The Roxy in West Hollywood. “This has been so beautiful. It’s a little sneak preview into Coachella, which is gonna be so much fun,” he told the small crowd. 15 Justin Bieber stripped down to just his shorts for his first public performance in four years at the Grammys in February. Getty Images for The Recording Academy But it’s been a slow burn to get Bieber back into the spotlight. In recent years, the 32-year-old has exhibited physical and mental woes — from crying in social media videos to worrying weight loss and temporary facial paralysis — as the toll of childhood fame has weighed heavily on him, even impacting his marriage to Hailey Bieber. “People keep telling me to heal … Don’t you think if I could have fixed myself I would have already?” the star posted online last summer following rants at the paparazzi. “I know I’m broken. I know I have anger issues.” 15 The volatile star yelled at waiting paparazzi as he left Soho House in Malibu in June. The Hollywood JR / BACKGRID 15 Bieber threw a bottle of water over a waiting photographer during a date night with wife Hailey Bieber last month. GAMR / BACKGRID And last month he was pictured flinging a bottle of water at a cameraman during a date night with Hailey. His most recent tour, in 2022, was cut short after he was diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome , a disorder that left him unable to control one side of his face in 2022. And 14 dates of his 2017 tour were canceled as he struggled with mental health issues. “Bieber has something to prove … to himself and to the industry,” said a well-placed music insider. “And Coachella will be a real indicator of how he moves forward. There’s a lot riding on this, no doubt.” 15 Two weeks before his Coachella comeback, Bieber performed at a surprise gig at The Roxy in West Hollywood. lilbieber/Instagram There are indeed a host of dragons Bieber has had to slay in making his stage comeback. The star had not performed in four years before his February appearance on stage at the Grammys, where he played “Yukon” while stripped down to his satin boxer shorts and a pair of socks. Bieber also seemingly referred to long-rumored issues in his marriage on the song “Walking Away” from his critically acclaimed seventh studio album, “Swag,” released last year: “Tell me why you’re throwing stones at my back/ You know I’m defenseless/ Girl, we better stop before we say some s–t/ We’ve been testing our patience/ I think we better off if we just take a break/ And remember what grace is.” 15 The “Baby” singer has made no secret of his emotional issues and was seen being comforted by now-wife Hailey Bieber in August 2018. MEGA 15 The star was visibly upset as he and Hailey drove to their pastor’s house in Beverly Hills in October 2018. Snorlax / MEGA And the track “Daisies” appeared to paint a picture of emotional uncertainty as he sang, “Do you love me or not? … You said ‘Forever,’ babe, did you mean it or not?” Page Six has learned Bieber spent years struggling financially, with his spending soaring to more than $1.3 million a month back in 2022, leaving his credit rating in the toilet. 15 Bieber’s latest albums, “Swag” and “Swag II,” have been parsed for lyrical clues about his marriage to Hailey Bieber. lilbieber/Instagram Well-placed sources told us that Bieber, who shares one-year-old son Jack Blues with Hailey, 29, was forced to sell his back catalog to Hipgnosis Songs Capital in January 2023 for a reported $200 million. While that’s typically something older artists do later in their career, we’re told that Bieber needed a cash infusion to pay for his $17 million mansion in La Quinta, California, and cover millions of dollars he owed to former manager Scooter Braun. Braun, who discovered Bieber when the singer was just 12, paid the debt — $26 million of a $40 million advance — to concert promoters AEG when 2022’s “Justice” tour was canceled. But the pair fell out when the singer, who agreed to pay back the loan over 10 years, reportedly only made one single payment that was not the full sum. 15 The couple share a one-year-old son, Jack Blues. justinbieber/Instagram 15 Jack was born on August 22, 2024. lilbieber/Instagram The battle ended last July when, according TMZ, Bieber agreed to repay the $26 million, as well as half of $11 million the music millionaire alleged Bieber owed for various unpaid commissions over the years. Despite many years of a close friendship as well as a working partnership, there is apparently no love lost. “Justin hates Scooter,” said another industry insider. “And so does Hailey.” Page Six has reached out to reps for the Biebers and Braun. 15 In April 2024, Bieber shared several candid, tearful photos on Instagram without explanation, causing concern among fans. Wife Hailey called him a “pretty crier.” lilbieber/Instagram TMZ reported in March that Bieber got into a heated argument with his childhood mentor, Usher, at Beyoncé and Jay-Z’s Oscars bash in Los Angeles. But this was denied to Page Six. Performing at Coachella is an intriguing prospect for Bieber’s future, the music insider said, as AEG runs the festival — and “This could be the precursor to a tour.” “This is a hard business,” said the insider, adding that this year’s festival is “the most in-demand Coachella in a long time … the first weekend is sold out” — with tickets getting snapped up in less than an hour. “It’s crazy. You have Bieber and Sabrina Carpenter [headlining the Friday shows] on the bill. Everyone is really curious to see what happens. 15 In 2024, Bieber drew attention for his gaunt frame, prompting his rep to deny drug use. GC Images “Clearly, this is the launch for a potential comeback tour. This is not a one-and-done situation,” the insider said. After the release of “Swag” and its follow-up, “Swag II,” Rolling Stone wrote: “Just when everyone was counting him out, Bieber surprised the world with the best music he’s ever made.” Tapping into his anger and trauma seemingly helped. On the “Therapy Session” skit with comedian Druski on “Swag,” Bieber confesses, “That’s been a tough thing for me recently, is feeling like I have had to go through a lot of my struggles as a human, as all of us do, really publicly. 15 The singer looked incredibly thin in 2024. In 2025, is rep said the past year had been “very transformative for him as he ended several close friendships and business relationships that no longer served him.” The Hollywood JR / BACKGRID “And so people are always asking if I’m OK, and that starts to really weigh on mе.” Extreme weight loss in 2024 left Bieber looking almost skeletal, worrying fans and prompting his rep to tell TMZ in 2025 that he was healthy and not using drugs — and note that the past year had been “very transformative for him as he ended several close friendships and business relationships that no longer served him.” Bieber has gone on to air all his issues in public, to the point where rumors have run rampant about his mental and physical stability. “I personally have always felt unworthy,” he posted on Instagram last March. “Like I was a fraud.” 15 Hailey, who has a billion-dollar beauty range, Rhode, will support her husband at Coachella later this month, sources tell Page Six. Diggzy/Shutterstock Days later, he vented about pent-up hatred from his past: “I was always told when I was a kid not to hate,” he wrote. “But it made me feel like I wasn’t allowed to hate it and so I didn’t tell anyone I’ve had it. Which made me feel like I have been drowning feeling unsafe to acknowledge it.” “If you don’t like my anger you don’t like me,” he then declared in June, posting screenshots of his texts raging at an unnamed target. “My anger is a response to pain I have been thru. Asking a traumatized person not to be traumatized is simply mean … I enjoyed our short lived relationship.” In April, Bieber launched his Skylrk clothing brand with a CGI video that depicted him walking into a house full of his old Drew House merch — and burning the place down. 15 Bieber celebrated Hailey’s 29th birthday on March 2. lilbieber/Instagram Last November, he was seen crying in a resurfaced Twitch stream as he sang about a doomed relationship, prompting fans to wonder if he was singing about his former girlfriend Selena Gomez. Meanwhile, his relationship with Hailey has stayed under the microscope. Last May, the “Baby” singer was vilified for a bizarre Instagram post when his wife was featured on the cover of Vogue . He admitted that, during a “huge fight,” he’d told her she would never be in Vogue, adding “Yikes I know, so mean.” 15 Hailey supported Bieber as he made his first public performance in four years at the Grammys. REUTERS Sharing that he said the words out of spite, the Grammy winner explained that “because I felt so disrespected I thought I gotta get even.” Hailey will be by her husband’s side when he takes the stage at Coachella. Despite their ups and downs, sources said, the couple are still in it. As crowds prepare to launch upon Palm Springs to see Bieber, the industry source said: “Coachella is not going to be ask you to headline if there’s no appetite … Justin is like Teflon. Despite everything, people still love him.” “He’s a talented kid,” added the music insider, “and people only want to see him succeed. It’s always hard when you [were once a teeny hopper and [then] want to be a legitimate music artist. But everybody wants him.”

title: Justin Bieber is headlining Coachella this month: How the pop star is forging a new path - AOL
description: After ending his "Justice" tour early and selling rights to his music catalog, the singer is entering a new era.
author: AOL
keywords: Justin Bieber, Coachella, Jack Blues Bieber, Hailey Bieber, Swag
text: This article contains affiliate links; if you click such a link and make a purchase, we may earn a commission. Kaitlin Reilly Fri, April 10, 2026 at 12:48 PM UTC 5 Justin Bieber is back. For the first time in his nearly two-decade-long career, the artist is headlining Coachella, performing on Saturday, April 11, and Saturday, April 18. The singer, who previously appeared on the Indio, Calif., music festival stage as a guest artist alongside acts like Tems , will reportedly receive a massive $10 million payday — a deal he supposedly negotiated directly with festival promoter Goldenvoice. Bieber's greatly anticipated Coachella performances come after a series of career highs — and lows. His 2025 two-part album, Swag , received four Grammy nominations and became a critical and commercial success. However, that same year, a series of interactions with paparazzi raised alarms over Bieber's health — audio from which Bieber included on Swag . (​​"It's not clocking to you that I'm standing on business, is it? I'm a human being, you're standing around my car, at the beach," Bieber says on the appropriately titled track "Standing on Business," featuring Druski.) Swag came after the "Yukon" artist ended his 2022 Justice tour early . He initially postponed dates, citing exhaustion and a desire to make health a priority. That same year, Bieber was diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome , which left his face partially paralyzed. The singer also parted ways with longtime manager Scooter Braun in 2023 after Braun stepped back from artist management, further suggesting a crossroads in Bieber's career. That same year, he sold the rights to his catalog of music released through 2021 to Hipgnosis Songs Capital, reportedly for more than $200 million . But now Coachella could mark a major milestone for the pop star. The father of Jack Blues Bieber has already been giving fans something to look forward to in performances leading up to the main event. Advertisement Justin Bieber played a surprise show in Los Angeles ahead of Coachella Before this month's music festival, Bieber stepped out for a series of intimate, invite-only performances in Los Angeles. In March, he performed at the Roxy Theatre, before hitting the stage at the Troubadour in April. At both shows, he sang songs exclusively off Swag , like "Daisies," "Yukon," "Go Baby" and "Devotion." Bieber shared videos of the performances on Instagram . Typically, Coachella has performers sign a (somewhat controversial) "radius clause," which prevents artists from performing in Southern California from the December before Coachella takes place through the May after the festival. The goal of the clause is to ensure that Coachella will benefit from the artist's placement on its lineup. However, artists can occasionally get around the clause or adjust it in their contract. Justin Bieber and Hailey Bieber at the 68th Grammy Awards. (Gilbert Flores/Billboard via Getty Images) While it's unclear whether Bieber's Los Angeles shows offer a preview of his entire Coachella setlist, it's worth noting that because Bieber sold the rights to his back catalog of music to all songs he released through 2021, he will not receive performance royalties for older tracks. Bieber recently performed at the Grammys Bieber's small shows in Los Angeles weren't his only performance ahead of Coachella: He also took the Grammys stage in February, where he stripped down to just shorts and socks to sing his Swag track "Yukon." His wife, Hailey Bieber, was emotional in the audience as Bieber showed off a back tattoo that was supposedly a portrait of the Rhode Beauty founder . The awards show's executive producer, Ben Winston, said that Bieber's rehearsal for the set moved quickly. "He came to the stage, he did it once," the producer told the podcast Rolling Stone Music Now after the show. "It was brilliant. We are seven minutes into his hour-and-a-half rehearsal. And he goes, 'How'd it look to you? You happy with it?' And I was like, 'Oh, my God. It looked beautiful. I loved it.' And he went, 'OK, fantastic. See you Sunday!'" Justin Bieber during the Grammys. (John Shearer/Getty Images for The Recording Academy) The stripped-down number was the first time Bieber performed at the Grammys in four years. In 2022, he was joined by Daniel Caesar and Giveon for a performance of "Peaches," off his 2021 album, Justice .

title: Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert | Coachella | The Guardian
description: Stars including Sabrina Carpenter, Karol G, David Byrne and Addison Rae also head to the desert for the first of two sold-out weekends of live music
author: https://www.theguardian.com/profile/benjamin-lee-film
text: Justin Bieber performs onstage. Photograph: John Shearer/Getty Images for The Recording Academy View image in fullscreen Justin Bieber performs onstage. Photograph: John Shearer/Getty Images for The Recording Academy This article is more than 1 month old Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert This article is more than 1 month old Stars including Sabrina Carpenter, Karol G, David Byrne and Addison Rae also head to the desert for the first of two sold-out weekends of live music Justin Bieber is set for a major live performance comeback at this year’s sold-out Coachella with rainy weather set to be a possible spoiler. The Canadian singer will face his biggest live stage since he abandoned his 2022 tour over health concerns. Bieber was experiencing “full paralysis” on one side of his face after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome. “I wish this wasn’t the case but obviously my body is telling me I gotta slow down,” he said at the time. Last year, he said the idea of touring again was “super daunting” to him and he was not planning to put on an official tour anytime soon. “I’m putting on a hell of a show for you guys for Coachella , getting ready, and getting inspired,” he also said to fans on Twitch. The annual music festival, which takes place in California’s Coachella valley, will host about 125,000 music lovers a day with seven stages offering a variety of artists including fellow headliners Sabrina Carpenter and Karol G. Other artists will include Moby, Geese, David Byrne, Addison Rae, the Strokes, Sombr, Iggy Pop, Ethel Cain and Labrinth over two weekends. Tickets sold out in just three days, an encouraging sign of fan interest for the second year in a row following 2024’s edition suffering from the slowest sales to date. View image in fullscreen Coachella in 2025. Photograph: Amy Harris/Invision/AP Coachella is also set to host a comeback for The xx , performing their first major festival after eight years ahead of their first album since 2017. Last week saw a surprise performance in Mexico City, announced as “the start of a next chapter together”. Carpenter, who performed at Coachella in 2024, has promised that her headlining set will be the “most ambitious” show she has ever done. “It’s probably the most time I’ve ever had to actually just sit down and talk about a show as I’m building it,” she said in an interview with Marc Jacobs this week. On Sunday, Karol G, the Colombian singer, will become Coachella’s first ever Latina headliner. She recently spoke about how she has been warned not to make any anti-ICE statements over fears it might affect her visa. “You become bait, because some people want to show their power,” she said in a Playboy interview. She also added that she is “willing to say” ICE out but “it’s something that crosses the line of what I have to do to protect myself”. This week’s schedule saw the surprise addition of Jack White while no-shows will include the punk duo Lambrini Girls after guitarist Phoebe Lunny announced on Instagram that she has “an acute brain injury”. Girl group Katseye member Manon will also miss the festival as she remains on a hiatus to “focus on her health and well-being”. After last year saw near-record temperatures, rising up to 102F (39C), which led to a rise in heat-related visits to nearby hospitals, this weekend sees the possibility of rain. Earlier reports of a potential thunderstorm have since been downgraded but weather will still see a drastic shift from 2025 highs. title: Kylie Jenner Arrives at Coachella Decked Out in Justin Bieber Merch - Newsweek
description: Justin Bieber will make his Coachella headlining debut on Saturday night, and Kylie Jenner came prepared for 'Bieberchella.'
text: By Megan Armstrong 0 Share Newsweek is a Trust Project member See more of our trusted coverage when you search. Prefer Newsweek on Google to see more of our trusted coverage when you search. Sabrina Carpenter will headline Coachella 2026 on Friday night, but Kylie Jenner is only at the Empire Polo Club in Indio, California, for one reason: to watch Justin Bieber headline on Saturday night. On Friday, Jenner posted an Instagram photo showing her triumphant Coachella arrival. In the photo, the self-made billionaire is wearing a sparkling tank top displaying Bieber throwing up two birds. Jenner also has two fully loaded SKYLRK bags, and she's standing in front of a SKYLRK-branded cart. Hailey Bieber, Justin's wife of more than seven years, has been extremely close with Kylie and Kendall Jenner since the early 2010s. Read More on Entertainment SKYLRK is Bieber's clothing and lifestyle brand, which will have an expansive presence on the Coachella grounds, according to Outlander Magazine . This weekend is paramount for Bieber because he is making his Coachella headlining debut, but it's more significant than that. Bieber parted ways with longtime manager Scooter Braun and his SB Projects management company sometime after the cancellation of his Justice World Tour in early 2023, and Braun retired as a music manager altogether in June 2024. We got our first taste of a totally free Bieber, creative or otherwise, when he released his genre-melding, Grammy-nominated album "SWAG" last July. Bieber doubled down on his newfound vision — or, really, his return to his pop/R&B roots — when he surprisingly dropped "SWAG II" last September. ... Bieber caught everyone's attention with his intentionally bare performance of "YUKON" at the 2026 Grammys in February. It was just a shirtless and shoeless Bieber, a guitar, a loop machine, and a microphone. In some ways, it felt symbolic. After being puppeteered by handlers and the industry since he was 13, Bieber doesn't want anything between him and the music. As one of the defining pop stars of his generation, Bieber played innumerable massive shows. His headlining 2022 Rock In Rio set drew over 100,000 people . Bieber's headlining Coachella sets on April 11 and April 18 will rival that capacity. The stage won't be too big. For one, as he reminded the world at the Grammys, Bieber's pure vocal talent is enough to captivate for hours. But most of all? Justin Bieber is finally singing for himself again. title: What time is Justin Bieber performing at Coachella?
description: Justin Bieber is set to headline Coachella 2026, marking his first billed performance after a recent period of personal growth.
text: Justin Bieber is a Coachella regular. Now's the right time to headline Ema Sasic Palm Springs Desert Sun Show Caption Hide Caption Coachella dates 2026, tickets, lineup, more must-know info Headed to the Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival 2026, or just planning to watch from home? Here's everything to know about this year's fest. As the start of Coachella 2026 draws near, many attendees are eagerly anticipating the return of one artist: Justin Bieber. When the pop star was announced as a headliner back in September, it drew a number of reactions. For many, it was thrilling because he's never been a billed performer on the lineup, previously only showing up as an attendee or special guest. It's also hard to believe it's his first Coachella set considering he was everywhere in the 2010s with hits such as "Baby," "Sorry" and Love Yourself." But this seems like the right time — 11:25 p.m. on Saturday, April 11, to be exact — for the singer to take the stage. More: Justin Bieber's best — and most infamous — Coachella Valley headlines After several years of constant media attention, Bieber recently took time away from the stage due to his health. That break, in turn, proved to be productive as he released "Swag" and "Swag II" last year, giving us an intimate look into his domestic life and how his musicality has matured since his teenage years. Bieber's headlining performance has the potential to be one of the most talked about and memorable sets from not just this festival, but of the decade. There's still much to wonder about his set, but some recent live appearances have given us some clues for what to expect. Bieber will take the Coachella stage Saturday, April 11, with the first weekend of the festival taking place Friday, April 10 through Sunday, April 12 at the Empire Polo Club in Indio. Past Coachella appearances draw excitement Though the 2026 festival marks the first time Bieber has been on the lineup, it's not the first time he's performed at Coachella. More: Billie Eilish? Gunna? Here's who could join Justin Bieber at Coachella Some of the artists he's guested with include Chance the Rapper in 2014 for the song "Confident"; 2019 headliner Ariana Grande to sing his hit "Sorry"; Daniel Caesar and Giveon in 2022 for a performance of "Peaches"; and Tems and Wizkid for the song "Essence." Each time he's appeared, the crowd's love and passion for him has been clear. Cheers and screams can be heard throughout the Empire Polo Club, signaling a superstar is in people's midst. Not to mention, Bieber's vocal chops only continue to get stronger and more soulful. Aside from on-stage appearances, Bieber has been a staple among the Coachella grounds. Fans have spotted him dancing with his wife, Hailey, hanging out with friends and even making Billie Eilish's dreams come true by meeting in person. However, it's also been reported that he punched a man in 2018 for choking a woman at an after party in La Quinta, so you never really know what you'll get with him. Why Coachella 2026 is the right time for Justin Bieber to headline Some fans might think Coachella organizers missed an opportunity in the 2010s to have Bieber on its lineup. "Bieber Fever" was all the rage then, and he consistently came out with chart-topping songs. But I reckon the Bieber we see on stage Saturday knows more about himself, the music he wants to put out and the show he wants to deliver than the Bieber we would have gotten a decade ago. The 2020s have seen a lot of change in Bieber's life. He and Hailey became parents in 2024, welcoming a son, and that has had a profound impact on the singer's personal life and work. It can be heard in his albums "Swag" and "Swag II," in which he explores married life and his journey into fatherhood. "When you take my hand, honey, I promise you just tenderness/Pull me out when you come in, and you turn me to a better man," he sings on "Better Man." In the song "Mother in You," Bieber croons about seeing so much of himself and his wife in their child with the lyrics, "My smile, her grace, both of our hearts." Those records have also shown the way he's matured when it comes to his music. Long gone are the ultra-poppy songs from his breakout years. Instead, listeners now get R&B and indie sounds with smooth vocals and infectious beats that paint vivid images. The 32-year-old has also been open about health struggles, addiction and the burden of fame over the years. At times, we've seen his more challenging moments — like when he clashed with paparazzi in Indio last year — but it's also humanized this figure who has been in the spotlight for nearly two decades now. What to expect from Justin Bieber's headlining set The big question remains: What is Bieber going to do when he hits the stage on Saturday? While we won't know for sure until his set begins, we've gotten some clues in recent weeks. The singer took the Grammy Awards stage in February — four years after his last performance of "Peaches" at the award show — and delivered a stripped-down version of "Yukon" from "Swag." He stepped out in lavender boxer shorts, black socks and a guitar, and while many artists opted for flashy production design, he was alone on stage with his music production equipment. Utilizing a looping station, Bieber built up the beat for his song before he let his soulful vocals take center stage. Throughout the performance, the singer kept his eyes closed and his hands close to his body, not at all acknowledging the crowd in front of him. It was a great reintroduction to the singer, who has taken a lengthy hiatus from performing after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome , which can cause facial weakness/paralysis. The Grammys set also showed that he doesn't need much to bring in an audience. Whenever the camera panned out into the crowd, people were captivated by the music and vibe he set. There's also the question of whether he'll stick to just his most recent work, or if he'll do a whole retrospective performance with some of his earlier hits at Coachella. In recent invite-only Los Angeles shows at The Roxy and Troubadour venues, the singer has opted for "Swag" and "Swag II" tracks, which leads many to believe they might not hear "Baby" after all. It's worth noting that Bieber sold his publishing and recorded music rights in 2022 for more than $200 million. The deal included his entire back catalog, totaling 290 songs released before Dec. 31, 2021. It's not clear if the sale would bar the singer from performing any of those songs, but we have a feeling he'd be allowed to sing them (it is Coachella, after all). Though there's many questions and speculation surrounding this performance, there's one thing we know to be true: Tens of thousands of festivalgoers will gather around the Coachella Stage to take in this decades-in-the-making performance. What day is Justin Bieber performing at Coachella? Saturday, April 11 and Saturday, April 18. Only Weekend 1 set times have been released so far, and for Weekend 1, his set will begin at 11:25 p.m. Coachella 2026 lineup Want to see who else is on this year's festival lineup? title: Coachella 2026: Set times finally arrive with Jack White a surprise addition – Orange County Register
description: Here are the Weekend 1 set times for Coachella 2026 so you know when to see Jack White, the Strokes, Addison Rae, Teddy Swims, KATSEYE, Laufey and more.
text: Coachella announces the addition of Jack White to the 2026 lineup for the annual music and arts festival. (Photo by Drew A. Kelley, Long Beach Press-Telegram/SCNG) By Peter Larsen | plarsen@scng.com | Orange County Register and Carolyn Burt | cburt@scng.com PUBLISHED: April 6, 2026 at 9:20 PM PDT | UPDATED: April 6, 2026 at 11:00 PM PDT Getting your Trinity Audio player ready... All day Monday the drumbeat grew louder and louder on the Coachella-themed social media sites: Where, oh where, are the set times? fans demanded. And then just after 8 p.m., like a mirage in the desert, there they were. The headliners for the 2026 Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival landed right about where you’d expect to find them. Sabrina Carpenter fills that slot on on Friday, April 10 on the main stage from 9:05-10:35 p.m., making her return to the Empire Polo Club after performing on the same stage though earlier in the day in 2024. Though Sabrina’s the headliner, she’ll be followed by the world premiere of techno artist Anyma’s immersive production titled “ÆDEN” at midnight. On Saturday, April 11 Justin Bieber tops the bill with his late-night set starting at 11:25 p.m. We close out Weekend 1 on Sunday, April 12, with headliner Karol G whose set begins at 9:55 p.m. This year’s set times also reveal several that some artists performance days have been switched from the original announcement. DJ Solomun announced recently that he had dropped off the bill for health reasons. And Foster the People was originally slated for Friday, April 10 but will now be performing on Sunday, April 12 at the Outdoor Theatre from 6:45-7:40 p.m. And Jack White showed up as a surprise act for weekend one, too. It’s been a theme in years past that with the set times being released, new artists are added to the line up. Last year, Weezer and Ed Sheeran were both added to the Mojave tent on Saturday, at 3 p.m. This year, White has been added for that same time frame from 3-3:45 p.m. in the Mojave tent for weekend one. It will be the fifth time White has played Coachella including appearances by his bands the White Stripes, Raconteurs and Dead Weather, and a solo headlining performance in 2015, his last time playing the desert fest. Another standout on the set list is less about the artists added and more about what’s missing. Long-time festivalgoers noticed a gap on Friday, April 10 in the Mojave tent from 9-10:30 p.m., around the same time headline Sabrina Carpenter takes the Coachella stage. Electronic artist Moby, the only act playing this 25th Coachella who was also in the lineup for the first one in 1999, finishes his set in the Mojave tent at 9 p.m. on Friday. The tent is dark until 10:35 p.m. when singer-songwriter Ethel Cain takes the stage. Could there be a surprise guest yet to be announced in that empty spot? Or could it involve a change to the Mojave stage to account for the announcement Blood Orange, who plays after Cain at 11:55 p.m. Friday, that he’d be performing in the round at Coachella? Time will tell. Coachella’s second weekend runs at the Empire Polo Club April 17-19. For the full lineup, now with set times, go to Coachella.com/schedule . Can’t wait for Coachella 2026? Check out our videos from last year to build the excitement of what’s to come. By Alex Ocho Updated on April 9, 2026 Share Image via Arturo Holmes/Getty Images for Coachella Key Takeaways Coachella 2026 streams for free on the official YouTube channel across both weekends (April 10–12 and 17–19), with multiview letting you watch up to four stages at once and switch audio between overlapping sets. The streams start at 7pm ET and 4pm PT Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber, KAROL G, Young Thug, BIGBANG, CLIPSE are the major headliners for this year's event. Coachella Merch from the festival and artists is available at the Complex Shop Coachella 2026 set times are officially out, and even if you're not heading to the desert, there are plenty of ways to tune in from home. The festival's livestream experience is expanding again this year, offering more stages, interactive features, and flexible viewing options so you can catch your favorite artists without missing a beat. How to watch the Coachella 2026 livestream from home You can watch performances via the official Coachella YouTube channel . The livestream runs across both festival weekends, April 10 to 12 and April 17 to 19, giving you access to multiple performances in real time across the festival’s seven stages. One of the biggest features is multiview, which allows you to watch up to four stages at the same time in multiview mode from your TV. While in multiview mode, you can switch audio between feeds so you can follow overlapping sets more easily. You can also use the Coachella livestream app, which is designed to make planning and watching easier. It lets you view the full livestream and replay schedule—synced to your time zone—so you know exactly when to tune in. You can use it to personalize your livestream schedule, set reminders, watch highlights on-demand, and more. Who is performing at Coachella 2026? The lineup includes major global names across both weekends, though it’s important to note this list reflects poster highlights and not every artist is guaranteed to be livestreamed. Friday, April 10 and April 17 includes Sabrina Carpenter, Disclosure, Teddy Swims, KATSEYE, Sexyy Red, Central Cee, and Foster the People. Saturday, April 11 and April 18 features Justin Bieber, The Strokes, Given, Addison Rae, Labrinth, SOMBR, Swae Lee, Taemin, PinkPantheress, Davido, and BIA. Sunday, April 12 and April 19 closes out the festival with KAROL G, Young Thug, BIGBANG, Major Lazer, FKA twigs, and CLIPSE. What should you know about set times and availability? Even though the set times are now available, livestream coverage is selective. The listed artists are pulled from the official poster and are not a guarantee of broadcast. A separate schedule for the YouTube broadcast will be made available on Coachella’s official website ahead of each festival day. Where can I buy official 2026 Coachella merchandise? title: Kylie Jenner Pays Tribute to Justin Bieber With Coachella 2026 Outfit
description: Kylie Jenner has returned to Coachella for another year—and literally dressed to support her longtime friend and festival headliner, Justin Bieber. See her look.
text: THE RUNDOWN Kylie Jenner is at Coachella weekend 1 and shared photos of her Justin Bieber-themed look on Friday. The star paired a sparkly tank top that had Bieber’s face on it with black pants and blue Skylrk shoes. The reality star has over a decade of history at the festival. Kylie Jenner has returned to Coachella for another year—and literally dressed to support her longtime friend, Justin Bieber, who is headlining the festival on Saturday night. For her outfit on the festival’s first day, Jenner paired a green tank top with Bieber’s face on it with black pants and blue Skylrk shoes. Skylrk is Bieber’s fashion brand. She posted her full look on Instagram Friday afternoon, captioning it, “chelly day 1!!!!!” She also teased her Bieberchella manicure on her Instagram Story: Instagram Instagram Instagram On TikTok, she shared more videos of herself getting ready in the look: View full post on TikTok View full post on TikTok Jenner has been close friends with Bieber for years. In 2015, she told ELLE UK , “I’m so close to him because I feel like he’s been through exactly what I’ve been through in a completely different way but very similar. So, he’s just...he just...he gets me, and every time I see him he always sits me down and is like, ‘Are you okay? You’re amazing. You can get through this, just stay true to yourself.’ He helps me a lot.” The star has over a decade of history at Coachella as well. Back in 2017, she spoke to ELLE about how she approached dressing for the occasion. “I think it takes a minute for me [to put an outfit together],” she said. “You’ll start thinking about it early, even if you change your mind 10 minutes before you get dressed. For me, I need to try and find a hair color that I haven’t done before. There’s not much left, but I’m committed to finding new options.” Years later, in 2024, Jenner gave ELLE an update on her style evolution more generally for her November cover story. “I think I’m actually going in the opposite direction [of minimalism],” she said. “I’ve definitely had some moments of quiet luxury, as they would say, but I’m always experimenting. At the root of my authentic style, I think I’m more dark feminine.” She credited the shift to the fact “I haven’t had a baby in a few years. The first part of my twenties was having children, learning what my personal style was and then losing it—not knowing how to dress, gaining 60 pounds for both pregnancies. It took me a year to feel like myself again. At 27, I feel more confident and more like myself than ever.”

title: Justin Bieber Teases Coachella Performance and Setlist
description: Justin Bieber gave fans a sneak preview into his setlist for Coachella 2026 during a private show over the weekend.
author: Maya Georgi
text: By Maya Georgi Maya Georgi Contact Maya Georgi on X Contact Maya Georgi by Email View all posts by Maya Georgi April 1, 2026 Justin Bieber Christopher Polk/Billboard/Getty Images Justin Bieber is prepping fans for his headlining Coachella set next week. On Tuesday, the pop star shared a montage clip on X from his secret pre-Coachella show at the Roxy Theater in Los Angeles this past weekend. Bieber performed his first U.S. concert in four years on Sunday night at an invite-only gig with a no-phones policy. The new clip provides fans who couldn’t be there a taste of what’s to come. In the clip, Bieber tells the Roxy Theater audience, “I just want to say thank you so much for coming tonight. This is so beautiful, it’s a little sneak preview into Coachella , which is going to be so much fun.” The clip also includes shots of the singer performing in a pink beanie and a TLC t-shirt at one point, and entirely shirtless at another. He’s seen cycling through Swag songs “Daisies,” “Speed Demon,” “Walking Away,” “Go Baby Go,” and “Yukon.” see you all soon pic.twitter.com/TFOV0jTDFv — Justin Bieber (@justinbieber) March 31, 2026 Bieber delivered a 25-song set on Sunday. Notably, the pop star only sang tracks from his two 2025 albums, Swag and Swag II (per Setlist.fm and a photo shared on social media). If the show and its accompanying setlist are a “sneak peek” into Bieber’s Coachella set, it could indicate that hits from the previous years, like 2021’s “Peaches” or 2010’s career-setting “Baby,” will not be performed. Trending Stories Jay-Z and Eminem to Reunite on Rakim Album With First Track Together in 25 Years Taylor Swift and Randy Newman Perform Duet of ‘You’ve Got a Friend in Me’ at ‘Toy Story 5’ Premiere Late-Night Hosts Take Aim at Trump’s Knicks Game Appearance: ‘He Hit Them With a Jinx’ Rush Return: Tears, Doublenecks, Monster New Drummer The private club gig also featured three separate sets, according to Setlist.fm, with two “regular” ones divided by an “acoustic set” in the middle of the performance. During the acoustic section, Bieber delivered tracks like “Mother in You,” “Things You Do,” and “Dotted Line.” Whatever he may have up his sleeve, Bieber’s Coachella performance is one of the most-anticipated sets from the festival. Prior to Sunday night’s invite-only event, the singer hadn’t performed a full-fledged public concert since his Justice tour ended in 2022. There have been a few scattered private performances, and Bieber did perform at the Grammys 2026 , but Coachella will mark his big return to a major stage for a set longer than a couple of songs.

title: Justin Bieber Coachella performance to pave way for greater surprise
description: Justin Bieber's Coachella performance is more than just a comeback, as it is expected to be a beginning of a new era for the pop star.The 31-year-old musician started his comeback to music with the surprise release of his album,...
text: Home latest pakistan world sports showbiz entertainment Royal business health Sci-Tech Food Fact Check big picture Videos shows Watch Entertainment Justin Bieber Coachella performance to pave way for greater surprise Justin Bieber's Coachella set has raised fans' expectations for pop star's comeback By Geo News Digital Desk | Published April 04, 2026 Justin Bieber's Coachella set has raised fans' expectations for pop star's comeback Justin Bieber’s Coachella performance is more than just a comeback, as it is expected to be a beginning of a new era for the pop star. The 31-year-old musician started his comeback to music with the surprise release of his album, SWAG , and then SWAG 2 , after a long hiatus since 2022. The Baby hitmaker will be headlining both the Saturdays in the music festival – April 11 and April 18 and it will mark one of his first big performances since he cancelled the tour midway in 2022 because of health issues. “Bieber has something to prove … to himself and to the industry. And Coachella will be a real indicator of how he moves forward. There’s a lot riding on this, no doubt,” an industry insider told Page Six. The Grammy winner also performed at the Grammys this year and sang his newly released song, Yukon, and according to the insider he has been preparing to launch a tour of his own after getting back on stage in front of his audience. During the years Bieber spent away from the spotlight, he suffered through many fallouts with collaborators and friends – like Scooter Braun, speculations about his health as he appeared sickly thin during his rare outings. However, the Daisies singer has returned and the insider noted that the Coachella performance “could be the precursor to a tour.” The source added, “This is a hard business,” adding that the festival this year is “the most in-demand Coachella in a long time … Clearly, this is the launch for a potential comeback tour. This is not a one-and-done situation.” Despite the lack of live performances from Bieber over the years, they noted that “He’s a talented kid…and people only want to see him succeed. It’s always hard when you [were once a teeny hopper and [then] want to be a legitimate music artist. Justin Bieber gave Beliebers (yes, we're still saying that in 2026) a taste of what to expect at Coachella 2026 by performing surprise, invite-only shows at The Roxy and The Troubadour in Los Angeles over the past two weeks. Bieber exclusively sang songs from "SWAG," his Grammy-nominated album from last July, and "SWAG II," its surprise counterpart released last September. For that reason, the working assumption has been that Bieber's headlining Coachella set would be similar to his warm-up shows. Read More Vansteenberghe posted "Justin Bieber's song list" with the disclaimer, "The rumor going around town." The purported set list looks like this: "Sorry" "With You" "So Sick" "Favorite Girl" "One Less Lonely Girl" "Baby" "I'm the One" "All That Matters" "Beauty and a Beat" "Where R U Now" "Daisies" "Yukon" "Devotion" "Love Song" "I Do" "Speed Demon" Bieber reposted Vansteenberghe's viral "Mommy? Mamacita" scene with Huda Mustafa on "Love Island USA" Season 7 last summer. Vansteenberghe and Olandria Carthen were fan favorites and finished as runners-up. "I just saw my face on his Instagram, and I know his new album dropped, too," Vansteenberghe told Decider last July. "Good things for JB. I love that he did that." When alerted that Bieber used the clip to help promote "SWAG," Vansteenberghe said, "Hell yeah. JB, I want to go to your concert." Fans will find out whether Vansteenberghe had real intel, which is plausible now that he is well-connected in Hollywood, or was just guessing like the rest of us when Bieber stages the most significant concert of his career this weekend. Bieber will make his Coachella headlining debut on Saturday, April 11, and headline again on Saturday, April 18. Other than his invite-only L.A. shows, the generational pop star most recently performed at the 2026 Grammys on Feb. 1. Bieber earned high praise for his stripped-back, looped rendition of "Yukon," his standout pop/R&B song from "SWAG." Bieber also live-streamed on Twitch throughout October and December, including everything from his Coachella rehearsals and casual jam sessions to a special performance of "Purpose" to celebrate the album's 10th anniversary.
